$comment: Default US payee/category → tax-line mapping ruleset (issue #23, M3-4). The classifier (lib/tax/classifyTransaction.mjs) overlays the user's rules from their tax profile (overrides.mappingRules, surfaced by the M3-1 loader at profile.mappingRules) ON TOP of these, then evaluates by ascending priority. PURELY DECLARATIVE data — no JavaScript, no executable logic. GENERIC and shareable: NOTHING here is specific to one taxpayer — no real business, account, lender, or category-group names. Per-user identifiers live in the profile INSTANCE (issue #25), never here. SCHEMA: assets/tax/mapping-rules.schema.json. MONEY UNITS: amountThresholdDollars and amountSign are evaluated in DOLLARS (YNAB milliunits / 1000), never raw milliunits. taxLineId values reference the bundled tax-line catalog (assets/tax/us-tax-lines.json, issue #21). A businessEntityId of "$profile" is a sentinel the classifier resolves to the owning business entity from the profile — it keeps owner-specific entity ids out of these defaults. Expense rules require amountSign:outflow so a refund/inflow at the same payee is never misclassified as a deductible expense. See docs/mapping-engine.md for precedence, tie-breaking, and the structural-signal mechanism.

rules.reason: Transaction posts to a business category-group or account; classifying as Schedule C other expenses for {businessEntityId}.
Payee '{payee}' matches developer-tools / SaaS / hosting / domain keywords (matched '{matchedKeyword}'); classifying as Schedule C other expenses.
Payee '{payee}' matches medical/dental keywords (matched '{matchedKeyword}'); classifying as Schedule A medical and dental expenses.
Payee '{payee}' matches mortgage/HELOC interest keywords (matched '{matchedKeyword}'); classifying as Schedule A home mortgage interest.
Payee '{payee}' matches state/local tax keywords (matched '{matchedKeyword}'); classifying as Schedule A state and local taxes.
Payee '{payee}' matches charitable/tithing keywords (matched '{matchedKeyword}'); classifying as Schedule A gifts to charity.
